16-year CEO tenure with disciplined M&A track record (21+ deals, no material write-downs)
Modular pre-fab capacity (3-4M sqft) and ~50-subsidiary labor pool create narrow execution moat
100%+ FCF conversion for four consecutive years; capex intensity only 1.7% of revenue

~42-45% of revenue from hyperscale data centers, concentrated in handful of customers

Beta 1.71 with stock +362% in 12 months — late-cycle pricing behavior
FCF conversion above 130% partly from customer-funded billings advances; reverses if backlog flattens

0 of 25 sensitivity grid cells support current price

Peak everything: peak margin, peak ROIC, peak FCF margin, peak multiple, peak backlog

Price below ~$805 (base IV) for real position sizing
Price below ~$497 (bear IV) for high-conviction buy

valuation_summary: Quant DCF intrinsic value range $497 (bear) / $805 (base) / $1,156 (bull) USD vs current price $1,991.50 — overvalued in every scenario by 71% to 301%, with zero of 25 sensitivity cells and 0.0% Monte Carlo probability supporting the price. No margin of safety at any defensible IV.
compact_checklist: Business: Comfort Systems is a roll-up of ~50 US specialty mechanical and electrical contractors that install and service HVAC, plumbing, piping, controls, and electrical systems in large buildings, with ~42% of FY2025 revenue now coming from hyperscale data centers and a $12.5B backlog providing roughly 12-15 months of forward visibility.

Moat: A narrow execution moat from ~3-4M sqft of modular pre-fabrication capacity, a labor pool across ~50 subsidiaries that takes years to replicate, and hyperscaler prequalification — real but partly cyclical, since roughly half of today's 14.4% operating margin is project-mix premium that reverts when the data-center cycle cools.

Economics: ROIC has tripled from 15.9% (FY22) to 47.5% (FY25) on 24.1% gross margin, 1.7%-of-revenue capex, and FCF conversion averaging ~128% of net income — extraordinary numbers, but mid-cycle ROIC is more plausibly 25-30% once operating leverage and working-capital tailwinds normalize.

Management: 16-year CEO Brian Lane has compounded revenue from $1.4B to $9.1B via 21+ disciplined bolt-on acquisitions with no material write-downs, a 14-year dividend-raise streak, and price-disciplined buybacks (only $2.5M YTD 2026), partially offset by ~$106M of insider net selling at prices below today's quote.

Debt risk: Fortress-grade — $499M net cash, 0.3x debt/EBITDA, 186x interest coverage, and a stress test (-30% revenue x 2 years at compressed margins) that the company survives with multiple years of liquidity runway, with the only real complexity being contractor billings dynamics and ~$2.3-3.2B of off-balance-sheet surety bonds.

Price: Today's $1,991 only makes sense if revenue keeps growing at 18-20%+ for 7+ more years AND operating margin expands from 14.4% to ~18% AND the exit multiple holds at 20-22x EV/EBITDA — three optimistic levers stacked simultaneously, outside the entire 5x5 sensitivity grid and Monte Carlo distribution (P(IV>Price) = 0.0%).

Margin of safety: None — bear/base/bull IV is $497/$805/$1,156 against a $1,991 price (MOS of -301%/-147%/-72%), zero of 25 sensitivity cells support the price, and a defensible starter entry only opens near $1,156 with real conviction not until $800 or below.

Thesis breaker: Sell or refuse to buy if operating margin compresses below 11% for two consecutive quarters, if data-center exposure exceeds 55% of revenue, or if backlog declines for two consecutive quarters — any one signals the hyperscaler-capex cycle has rolled over.
